prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `a2d730bd`.
codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `a2d730bd` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a2d730bd/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a2d730bd/verifier.py):4, and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a2d730bd/generator.py):162. The rule is: for each non-background color, find the main solid rectangle, then for each same-colored singleton, erase the singleton and draw the straight connector to the nearest aligned side, with orthogonal caps at both ends. I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint as a starting hypothesis because it matched the official examples; no new primitive was needed and no `notes.md` was necessary.

Validation passed end to end: `4/4` official examples, `50/50` generator smoke checks, and `1000/1000` generated examples via `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task a2d730bd`. The generator stays in-family by sampling 1-3 large horizontal/vertical rectangles plus aligned singleton markers and reserving the future connector footprints so outputs remain sparse and clean. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a2d730bd/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a2d730bd/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a2d730bd/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a2d730bd/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a2d730bd/previews/preview_03.png).
